title: 24:37 ГўВҖВ” September 23, 2024
body_text:
24:37 ГўВҖВ” September 23, 2024
Update to the Wires Part
We updated the Wires Part to reflect a process change for wires alerted as potential fraud in the Enterprise Fraud Management System (EFM). We also made other minor changes to the Part, including an update to Wires Customization Request, Form 6493. See the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information on EFM.
Electronic Transactions Volume
Wires Part
Section 1.3, Wires Availability
We added that wire processing is subject to cut-off times and added a reference to Section 14.7, Cut-off times for Outgoing Wires, for more information.
Section 3.3, Establishing Policies and Procedures for Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM)
We removed references to wires alerted as ГўВҖВңAuto RiskГўВҖВқ in EFM due to a process change. Now all wires that are alerted in EFM will have a ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ status in the Wires application until they are actioned in EFM.
Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements
We updated the description of the Collect Funding Method option to include the new question asking if the funds were collected by another person or entity. If the user selects ГўВҖВңYesГўВҖВқ, they must enter the individual/entity details.
Section 4.3, Requesting Wires Customization Changes
We updated the image for Wires Customization Request, Form 6493.
Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM)
We removed references to wires alerted as ГўВҖВңAuto RiskГўВҖВқ in EFM due to a process change. Now all wires that are alerted in EFM will have a ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ status in the Wires application until they are actioned in EFM. We added a note that Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt indicate why an outgoing wire has a pending status and that authorized EFM users can access details for wires that are under investigation for potential fraud.
Section 14.7, Cut-off times for Outgoing Wires
We clarified that for outgoing wires to be settled with Central 1 (not the destination) the same business day, they must be approved before the cut-off time. We also clarified that wires are monitored for fraud and scanned against compliance lists and can be held for investigation. We made other editorial changes.
Section 22.2, Approving Outgoing Wires
We added a note that approved outgoing wires canГўВҖВҷt be cancelled in the Wires application. Financial institutions may be able to submit a trace request to cancel a future-dated wire or submit a recall or amendment request. These are done on a best efforts basis and arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed.
Section 23.1, Overview of Cancelling Outgoing Wires
We added a note that Wires doesnГўВҖВҷt indicate why an outgoing wire was cancelled. Authorized EFM users can access details for wires cancelled due to fraud. We removed references to wires alerted as ГўВҖВңAuto RiskГўВҖВқ in EFM due to a process change. Now all wires that are alerted in EFM will have a ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ status in the Wires application until they are actioned in EFM.
Section 26.1, Overview of Settlement
We clarified that settlement is subject to wire processing cut-off times and referred users to Section 14.7, Cut-off times for Outgoing Wires, for more information.

Wires Customization Request
6493
We updated the Collect Funding Method section to include the new source of funds fields.
Revised
